      "それは、ゲームボードを持っています。",
      "それは、特別なカードを持っています。",
      "それは、ホームで終わる1〜20の番号が付いた80個の穴の境界線を持つ正方形のボードと、プレイヤーごとに4つの追加の穴を持っています。",
      "それは、ボードのサイズに応じてゴルフティーまたはクリベッジペグであるプレイングピースを持っています。",

      "それは、プレイヤーがゲームボード上でトークンを競走させるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、サイコロではなくトランプでプレイされるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、すべてのトークンを「ホーム」に持ち帰る最初のプレイヤーになるという目的を持つという特徴があります。",
      "それは、クロスアンドサークルゲームであるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、町から町、家から家へと変化する特別なカードを持つという特徴があります。",
      "それは、開始フィールドにあるトークンによって作成されるブロックという特徴があります。",
      "それは、一部のカードに特別な機能があり、ゲームをより挑戦的で興味深いものにするという特徴があります。",
      "それは、ホームを上から下へと満たす必要があるという特徴があります。",
      "それは、チームベースのバリアントで、チームメンバーが互いにカードを交換するという特徴があります。",
      "それは、チームメンバーがすべてのトークンをホームに持ち帰ると、パートナーのトークンを動かすのを助けるという特徴があります。"

About: It is about a board game in which players race their four tokens around the game board from start to finish.
Affects: It affects opponents tokens by replacing them when landed on.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Ludo, Aggravation or Sorry!.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Cross and Circle game family.
ConsistsOf: It consists of four tokens or marbles per player.
CreatedIn: It was created in Quebec, Canada.
CreatedTo: It was created to be a board game where players race their tokens.
HasA: It has tokens or marbles.
It has playing cards.
It has a game board.
It has specialty cards.
It has square boards with a border of 80 holes numbered 1-20 ending at home with an additional 4 holes per player.
It has playing pieces that are either golf tees or cribbage pegs.
It has a starting field.
It has a house to be filled from the top down.
It has team based modes.
It has common color tokens in an extended two versus two players variant.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by players racing their tokens around the game board.
It is characterized by being played with playing cards rather than dice.
It is characterized by the objective of being the first to take all of one's tokens home.
It is characterized by being a Cross and Circle game.
It is characterized by specialty cards that vary from town to town, household to household.
It is characterized by a blockade created by a token on its starting field.
It is characterized by many variations where some cards have special functions.
It is characterized by the requirement to fill home from the top down.
It is characterized by team members exchanging one card with each other in team based variations.
It is characterized by the ability to help move partners' tokens once a team member has brought all their tokens home.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the French game Jeu des petits chevaux.
IsA: It is a Cross and Circle game.
It is a team based game.
KnownAs: It is known as Tuck in some English parts of Quebec and Atlantic Canada.
It is known as Pock in some parts of Alberta.
It is known as Jeu du Toc in France.
It is known as Jeu des petits chevaux canadiens in France.
It is known as TUK in Vermont.
It is known as TUC in Vermont.
KnownFor: It is known for its gameplay similar to Ludo, Aggravation or Sorry!.
PartOf: It is part of the Cross and Circle game family.
UsedAs: It is used as a starter card.
It is used as a blockade.
It is used as a common color of tokens in an extended two versus two players variant.
UsedFor: It is used for racing tokens around a game board.
It is used for moving tokens around the board.
It is used for capturing opponent tokens.
It is used for swapping tokens.
It is used for moving tokens to the starting field.
It is used for creating blockades.
It is used for moving tokens backward.
It is used for distributing moves among all tokens.
It is used for moving tokens home.
It is used for helping move partners' tokens.
UsedIn: It is used in team based modes.
It is used in Saskatchewan variant.
It is used in Cape Breton variant.
It is used in Vermont variant.
It is used in two versus two players standard.
It is used in three versus three players.
It is used in two versus two players extended.
It is used in two vs. two vs. two players.
It is used in three vs three with captains.
It is used in a 54-Cards Game.
UsedTo: It is used to move a new token to the starting field.
It is used to move tokens around the board.
It is used to replace an opponent's token.
It is used to swap one of their own tokens with any other token on the circle track.
It is used to move tokens backward.
It is used to make individual moves of one field.
It is used to distribute moves among all tokens.
It is used to move tokens home.
It is used to help move partners' tokens.
It is used to exchange cards with team members.
